Traffic Mirror Lifecycle Flow
hyperedges.description: End-to-end flow: ASG lifecycle events trigger Lambda handler which creates or deletes VPC traffic mirror sessions to maintain full coverage of auto-scaled EKS nodes.

hyperedges.label: Suricata Inspection Pipeline
hyperedges.description: Traffic from bc-prd EKS node ENIs is mirrored through filter and NLB target to Suricata NIDS for network intrusion detection.

hyperedges.label: Idempotent Session Management Pattern
hyperedges.description: Both create and delete paths implement idempotency: create checks for existing session before creating, delete handles already-gone sessions gracefully.

nodes.label: auto_mirror Lambda
nodes.description: Lambda function that dynamically manages VPC Traffic Mirror Sessions for auto-scaled EKS nodes in bc-prd. Triggered by EventBridge on ASG lifecycle events.

nodes.label: handler()
nodes.description: Lambda entry point. Routes EventBridge events to create_session or delete_session based on detail-type.

nodes.label: create_session()
nodes.description: Creates a VPC traffic mirror session for a newly launched EKS node ENI. Idempotent — skips if session already exists.

nodes.label: delete_session()
nodes.description: Deletes mirror session for a terminating instance. Falls back to tag-based lookup if ENI already detached.

nodes.label: is_eks_node()
nodes.description: Checks if instance belongs to EKS_CLUSTER_NAME by inspecting eks:cluster-name tag.

nodes.label: get_primary_eni()
nodes.description: Returns primary ENI (device-index 0) for an instance. Retries with backoff because ENI may not be visible immediately at launch.

nodes.label: find_session_for_eni()
nodes.description: Looks up existing TrafficMirrorSession by ENI ID and target ID.

nodes.label: _delete()
nodes.description: Low-level delete of a mirror session by ID. Idempotent — ignores InvalidTrafficMirrorSessionId.

nodes.label: EventBridge ASG Lifecycle Events
nodes.description: EventBridge rules matching EC2 Instance Launch Successful and EC2 Instance Terminate Successful events from Auto Scaling.

nodes.label: VPC Traffic Mirroring
nodes.description: AWS VPC Traffic Mirroring — copies network traffic from ENIs to a mirror target (NLB) for inspection by Suricata.

nodes.label: Traffic Mirror Filter
nodes.description: aws_ec2_traffic_mirror_filter resource managed by Terraform, passed to Lambda via MIRROR_FILTER_ID env var.

nodes.label: Traffic Mirror Target (NLB)
nodes.description: aws_ec2_traffic_mirror_target backed by an NLB that routes mirrored traffic to Suricata DaemonSet. Passed via MIRROR_TARGET_ID.

nodes.label: Suricata NIDS DaemonSet
nodes.description: Suricata network IDS running as DaemonSet in bc-prd, receiving mirrored traffic via NLB target.

nodes.label: bc-prd EKS Cluster
nodes.description: Production EKS cluster. Auto-scaled nodes get traffic mirror sessions via this Lambda.

nodes.label: traffic-mirroring.tf
nodes.description: Terraform config that creates static mirror sessions for existing nodes + the Lambda + EventBridge rule for auto-scaled nodes.

links.evidence: Lambda filters by EKS_CLUSTER_NAME to only mirror nodes in the production EKS cluster
Terraform traffic-mirroring.tf creates the Lambda + EventBridge rule, passes env vars (MIRROR_FILTER_ID, MIRROR_TARGET_ID, EKS_CLUSTER_NAME)
handler() calls create_session(instance_id) on 'EC2 Instance Launch Successful' events
handler() calls delete_session(instance_id) on 'EC2 Instance Terminate Successful' events
Docstring states Lambda is triggered by EventBridge on Auto Scaling lifecycle events
create_session checks is_eks_node(instance_id) before creating mirror
create_session calls get_primary_eni(instance_id) to find the source ENI
create_session calls find_session_for_eni(eni_id) for idempotency check
create_session calls ec2.create_traffic_mirror_session() to create mirror sessions
delete_session calls find_session_for_eni(eni_id) to locate session to delete
delete_session calls _delete(session_id, eni_id) to perform actual deletion
Checks eks:cluster-name tag matches EKS_CLUSTER_NAME to confirm node belongs to the cluster
_delete calls ec2.delete_traffic_mirror_session() to remove mirror sessions
traffic-mirroring.tf creates the mirror filter resource referenced by Lambda env var
NLB target routes mirrored VXLAN traffic to Suricata DaemonSet (inferred from architecture docs and description field)
traffic-mirroring.tf creates the mirror target (NLB) resource referenced by Lambda env var
